soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The statement invokes a common 'efficiency-bound object' for the profiled minimax selected ATE, but §6/§7 define only finite first-order influence functions, not the semiparametric model, tangent space, or variance functional that makes an efficiency bound well-defined.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Define the efficiency-bound object explicitly as either Var_P(psi_{j,Q,P}) in a finite nonparametric model or a projected semiparametric bound with a named tangent space, then restate Conjecture 1 in those terms.

Add one local positive-threshold clause to Conjecture 1 that separates 'different singleton native ATEs' from 'non-singleton interval but all SPD selectors accidentally choose the same value' to make the iff direction visibly non-tautological.
Add NeweyPowell2003 or a comparable NPIV conditional-moment source to the related-work checklist as the classical bridge-equation/inverse-problem ancestor behind the Bennett and Darolles comparisons.

recommended_next_step: Revise the efficiency-bound terminology in Conjecture 1 and §7; the novelty kernel otherwise survives the repo and published-axis checks.
